summary.narrative: Gov. Green campaigned on homelessness reduction, housing development, and cost-of-living relief. Administration claims significant progress: $1.5B rainy day fund, 253 bills signed in 2024, housing development at 'scale not seen in decades', and homelessness reduction target by 2026. Tax cuts implemented 2024; future cuts paused 2027-2029.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong fiscal management and legislative productivity demonstrated. Housing and homelessness initiatives show tangible progress but require longer-term outcome validation. Fiscal discipline (spending reductions without service cuts) is verifiable. Tax cut execution clear but future pause creates policy uncertainty.

promises.normalized: Address homelessness in Hawaii with comprehensive plan; cut homelessness in half by 2026
Build new housing at scale not seen in decades; expand residential development significantly
Reduce cost of living in Hawaii through fiscal management and economic policy
Implement income tax cuts in 2024; pause future cuts 2027-2029 pending fiscal conditions

data_gaps: Specific homelessness reduction metrics and current vs. target numbers
Housing units constructed to date vs. projected targets
Impact assessment of 253 bills signed in 2024
Details on Maui wildfire recovery progress and funding
Specific executive order outcomes and measurable effects
Public approval ratings and polling data
Detailed breakdown of $1.5B rainy day fund allocation plans
